mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, recession probabilities rise sharply, triggering risk-off positioning that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in over 60% of periods where INDPRO increases, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the conditional return distribution shows no significant negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows, or forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend signals sustained aggregate demand expansion, which flows through to corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of above-trend GDP quarters, or the signal fails to maintain a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side contraction, which immediately dampens equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of periods following INDPRO declines, or the negative correlation coefficient falls below -0.05 in out-of-sample testing.
